mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over all rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and force negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a broad consumer demand collapse that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient economic activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 270.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 270.0, invalidating the inflation threshold effect.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate signals labor force participation deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE increases shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or fails to predict negative 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strengthening aggregate demand that boosts corporate revenue growth and equity earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 for 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates lingering supply chain bottlenecks that constrain output growth and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases, with no statistical significance.
